key_points: Residential system mentioned positively; specific details scarce in data
Campus has underground tunnels rumored among applicants
No major housing complaints surfaced in available student data
key_quotes: beautiful campus, less pretentious, residential system is good
Colgate has some sweet underground tunnels, I hear. Can anyone verify?

key_points: Greek life is a dominant social force; students ask about it immediately
Isolation is a real concern — winter activities fill the social void
Multiple students asked about weekend parties, suggesting limited alternatives
key_quotes: How big is greek life on campus??
It's very cold and isolated. I'm afraid I'll get bored since I don't like outdoor winter activities, which I believe the students do to pass the time.
Can you please make a video on a typical weekend at Colgate?! The parties and stuff to enjoy around.

key_points: Colgate famously mailed students actual pizza — marketing stunt or genuine culture?
No substantive student complaints or praise about dining hall food found
Pizza mailing generated buzz but zero info on day-to-day food quality
key_quotes: Idc how gross it looks, if i get mailed a pizza there's no way I'm not tasting it
what if they're gone for the weekend? then the pizza's just... sitting there...

key_points: Isolation and harsh winters flagged as mood and wellbeing risks
No mention of counseling services or mental health support in any post
Students from warmer states openly worried about fitting in and loneliness
key_quotes: I live in North Carolina which is one thing keeping me from fully considering it
I am from california. And it's a big decision to move to the other coast.
Is Colgate fun as an asian, I'm a south Asian and I'm so scared of facing racism and loneliness there

key_points: Gives zero merit aid — only need-based grants, hurts upper-middle-class families
Strong financial aid for lower-income students; some report near-full grants
International students should not expect need-blind treatment here
key_quotes: Colgate University- accepted with 0k in merit and grants (they only give financial grants)
and they couldn't accept me because i needed aid???????
I have a 51k Grant for Colgate, which will (probably) make it cheaper than Mich.

key_points: Small class sizes and close professor relationships praised repeatedly for pre-med
Grade inflation acknowledged as a real advantage over larger research universities
CS program is weak for a school of this caliber; LAC format limits STEM depth
key_quotes: Small class sizes, close relationships with professors will help you get many meaningful research experiences and awesome letters of recs.
Colgate easy. You don't want to deal with grade deflation and risk having to go to the Caribbean.
These are all liberal arts schools so they may not be the best for that specific field.

key_points: Admissions office runs aggressive and creative outreach — pizza, puzzles, videos mailed
Fee waiver promotions suggest they actively chase application volume
No student complaints about admin responsiveness surfaced in the data
key_quotes: First Colgate sent a whole video in the mail, then they sent puzzle pieces, and now they're sending pizza??
They're playing 4D chess. They know exactly what's going to happen here: This is so weird, people are of course going to share it and talk about it.
Got this email too... gotta say, it actually worked. I don't think I was going to apply but hey!

key_points: Hamilton, NY is genuinely isolated — this is the single biggest recurring concern
Beautiful campus consistently praised; described as less pretentious than peer schools
Outdoor activities (hiking, lakes) are the main local draw; useless if you hate winter
key_quotes: It's very cold and isolated. I'm afraid I'll get bored since I don't like outdoor winter activities.
Is there anything to do in Hamilton? Either there is nothing to do or lots to do. Hiking, bicycling, lakes, rivers. It's not NYC however.
beautiful campus, less pretentious, residential system is good, giant robot arm in library

key_points: Strong pre-med track with high med school placement implied by grade inflation culture
No specific data on finance, consulting, or tech recruiting pipelines
3-2 engineering program exists but sends students elsewhere for the technical degree
key_quotes: Colgate, it gives you a less stressful track into med school. The classes are smaller, there's grade inflation.

Id say Colgate. Small more intimate classes and greater interaction with professors will help you get many meaningful research experiences.
